docs.title: OTP
docs.text: Standalone OTP and MFA primitives for PHP.

docs.title: What this library includes
docs.text:
• TOTP (RFC6238)
  • HOTP (RFC4226)
  • OCRA (RFC6287)
  • Generic OTP backed by PSR-6 cache storage
  • Recovery and backup codes
  • Replay protection contracts and in-memory stores
  • otpauth:// generation and parsing

docs.title: Why this package
docs.text:
This package aims to cover the practical OTP and MFA building blocks that applications usually need, without turning into a full authentication framework.

It gives you:

  • simple boolean verification methods when that is enough
  • richer result objects when you need drift, replay, or matched-step details
  • provisioning helpers for authenticators
  • pluggable storage contracts for replay state and recovery codes

docs.title: ReplayStoreInterface
docs.text: Use this to persist replay state for TOTP, HOTP, or OCRA verification flows.

docs.title: RecoveryCodeStoreInterface
docs.text: Use this to persist hashed recovery codes and their metadata.

docs.title: SecretStoreInterface
docs.text: Use this when building your own secret storage abstraction around the package.

docs.title: Included in-memory stores
docs.text:
• Infocyph\OTP\Stores\InMemoryReplayStore
  • Infocyph\OTP\Stores\InMemoryRecoveryCodeStore

docs.title: VerificationResult
docs.text:
Used by advanced verification flows in TOTP, HOTP, and OCRA.

Fields:

  • matched
  • reason
  • matchedTimestep
  • matchedCounter
  • driftOffset
  • replayDetected
  • verifiedAt

docs.title: RecoveryCodeGenerationResult
docs.text:
Fields:

  • plainCodes
  • totalGenerated
  • remainingCount
  • lastUsedAt

docs.title: RecoveryCodeConsumptionResult
docs.text:
Fields:

  • consumed
  • reason
  • remainingCount
  • totalGenerated
  • lastUsedAt

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: • PHP 8.4 or newer

docs.title: Package installation
docs.text: composer require infocyph/otp

docs.title: Documentation toolchain
docs.text:
This project uses Material for MkDocs.

The official Material for MkDocs installation guide recommends installing with pip, ideally in a virtual environment, and pinning to the current major version. It also suggests generating a lockfile with pip freeze for reproducible builds.

Official reference:

  • https://squidfunk.github.io/mkdocs-material/getting-started/

Example setup:

pip install mkdocs-material=="9.*"
pip freeze > docs/requirements.txt

Install from the lockfile:

pip install -r docs/requirements.txt

docs.title: Local docs preview
docs.text: mkdocs serve

docs.title: Test suite
docs.text: php vendor/bin/pest

docs.title: Generic OTP
docs.text:
• Generic OTP codes are now strings, not integers.
  • Leading zeroes are preserved.
  • The generic OTP class now expects a caller-provided PSR-6 cache pool.
  • Digit count is validated as OTP digits, not PHP integer size.

docs.title: TOTP
docs.text:
• The old boolean leeway model has been replaced with configurable past and future windows.
  • verify() still gives you a boolean API.
  • verifyWithWindow() returns a VerificationResult for richer handling.

docs.title: HOTP
docs.text:
• verify() now supports look-ahead.
  • verifyWithResult() returns matched counter and drift metadata.

docs.title: Provisioning
docs.text:
• otpauth:// parsing is now supported.
  • Enrollment payload helpers expose URI, QR payload, and optional SVG.
  • Label and issuer handling is centralized and stricter.

docs.title: Recovery codes and replay protection
docs.text:
• Recovery codes are first-class package features now.
  • Replay protection is pluggable through contracts and in-memory stores.

docs.title: TOTP
docs.text:
use Infocyph\OTP\TOTP;

$secret = TOTP::generateSecret();

$totp = (new TOTP($secret))
    ->setAlgorithm('sha256');

$otp = $totp->getOTP();
$isValid = $totp->verify($otp);

Advanced verification with a drift window:

use Infocyph\OTP\Stores\InMemoryReplayStore;
use Infocyph\OTP\ValueObjects\VerificationWindow;

$result = $totp->verifyWithWindow(
    $otp,
    timestamp: time(),
    window: new VerificationWindow(past: 1, future: 1),
    replayStore: new InMemoryReplayStore(),
    binding: 'user-42',
);

$result->matched;
$result->matchedTimestep;
$result->driftOffset;
$result->replayDetected;

docs.title: HOTP
docs.text:
use Infocyph\OTP\HOTP;

$secret = HOTP::generateSecret();

$hotp = (new HOTP($secret))
    ->setCounter(3)
    ->setAlgorithm('sha256');

$otp = $hotp->getOTP(346);
$isValid = $hotp->verify($otp, 346);

docs.title: Generic OTP
docs.text:
use Infocyph\OTP\OTP;
use Psr\Cache\CacheItemPoolInterface;

/** @var CacheItemPoolInterface $cachePool */
$otp = new OTP(
    digitCount: 6,
    validUpto: 60,
    retry: 3,
    hashAlgorithm: 'xxh128',
    cacheAdapter: $cachePool,
);

$code = $otp->generate('signup:alice@example.com');
$otp->verify('signup:alice@example.com', $code);

docs.title: Recovery codes
docs.text:
use Infocyph\OTP\RecoveryCodes;
use Infocyph\OTP\Stores\InMemoryRecoveryCodeStore;

$codes = new RecoveryCodes(new InMemoryRecoveryCodeStore());
$generated = $codes->generate('user-42');
$consumed = $codes->consume('user-42', $generated->plainCodes[0]);

docs.title: Benchmarks
docs.text: The project includes a phpbench configuration and a starter benchmark suite under benchmarks/.

docs.title: Available commands
docs.text:
Run the default aggregate report:

composer bench:run

Run a quicker local iteration:

composer bench:quick

Render the chart-style console report:

composer bench:chart

docs.title: What is benchmarked
docs.text:
The starter suite in benchmarks/OtpBench.php covers:

  • TOTP generation
  • TOTP verification
  • TOTP verification with replay state
  • HOTP generation
  • HOTP verification
  • OCRA generation
  • OCRA verification
  • Generic OTP generation
  • Generic OTP verification

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• benchmarks are comparative measurements, not absolute guarantees
  • for stable numbers, prefer repeated iterations on a quiet machine
  • replay-aware benchmarks create fresh in-memory state for cleaner runs

docs.title: Extending the suite
docs.text:
Add more *Bench.php files under benchmarks/.

The current benchmark config uses:

  • runner.path = benchmarks
  • runner.file_pattern = *Bench.php
  • attribute-based benchmark discovery

docs.title: Generic OTP
docs.text: Generic OTP is useful when you want one-time codes without managing a dedicated OTP database table, while still keeping server-side verification state.

docs.title: PSR-6 cache requirement
docs.text:
use Infocyph\OTP\OTP;

$otp = new OTP(
    digitCount: 6,
    validUpto: 60,
    retry: 3,
    hashAlgorithm: 'xxh128',
    cacheAdapter: $cachePool,
);

docs.title: Codes are strings
docs.text:
• generated codes are strings
  • verification expects strings
  • leading zeroes are preserved

docs.title: Basic flow
docs.text:
$code = $otp->generate('signup:alice@example.com');
$otp->verify('signup:alice@example.com', $code);
$otp->delete('signup:alice@example.com');

docs.title: Retry semantics
docs.text:
$otp->verify('signup:alice@example.com', $code, deleteIfFound: false);

When deleteIfFound is false, the record remains until it is verified, expires, or runs out of retries.

docs.title: Basic usage
docs.text:
use Infocyph\OTP\HOTP;

$hotp = (new HOTP($secret))
    ->setCounter(3)
    ->setAlgorithm('sha1');

$otp = $hotp->getOTP(10);
$hotp->verify($otp, 10);

docs.title: Look-ahead verification
docs.text:
HOTP often needs controlled counter resynchronization.

$hotp->verify($otp, counter: 10, lookAhead: 5);

docs.title: Rich verification result
docs.text:
use Infocyph\OTP\Stores\InMemoryReplayStore;

$result = $hotp->verifyWithResult(
    $otp,
    counter: 10,
    lookAhead: 5,
    replayStore: new InMemoryReplayStore(),
    binding: 'device-1',
);

$result->matched;
$result->matchedCounter;
$result->driftOffset;
$result->replayDetected;

docs.title: Basic generation and verification
docs.text:
use Infocyph\OTP\OCRA;

$ocra = new OCRA('OCRA-1:HOTP-SHA256-8:C-QN08-PSHA1', $sharedKey);
$ocra->setPin('1234');

$code = $ocra->generate('12345678', 0);
$ocra->verify($code, '12345678', 0);

docs.title: Optional suite inputs
docs.text:
Depending on the suite, you may need:

  • setPin()
  • setSession()
  • setTime()

docs.title: Parsed suite details
docs.text:
$suite = $ocra->getSuite();

$suite->algorithm;
$suite->digits;
$suite->counterEnabled;
$suite->challengeFormat;
$suite->challengeLength;
$suite->optionals;

docs.title: Replay protection
docs.text:
use Infocyph\OTP\Stores\InMemoryReplayStore;

$result = $ocra->verifyWithResult(
    $code,
    challenge: '12345678',
    counter: 0,
    replayStore: new InMemoryReplayStore(),
    binding: 'user-42',
);

docs.title: Provisioning URI
docs.text: $uri = $totp->getProvisioningUri('alice@example.com', 'Example App');

docs.title: SVG QR rendering
docs.text: $svg = $totp->getProvisioningUriQR('alice@example.com', 'Example App');

docs.title: Enrollment payload
docs.text:
$payload = $totp->getEnrollmentPayload(
    'alice@example.com',
    'Example App',
    withQrSvg: true,
);

$payload->secret;
$payload->uri;
$payload->qrPayload;
$payload->qrSvg;

docs.title: Parse existing otpauth:// URIs
docs.text:
use Infocyph\OTP\TOTP;

$parsed = TOTP::parseProvisioningUri($uri);

$parsed->type;
$parsed->secret;
$parsed->label;
$parsed->issuer;

docs.title: Issuer and label behavior
docs.text:
Label formatting is centralized to avoid malformed outputs such as duplicated issuer prefixes.

The provisioning layer:

  • normalizes issuers
  • safely formats labels
  • separates URI generation from QR rendering

docs.title: Generating codes
docs.text:
use Infocyph\OTP\RecoveryCodes;
use Infocyph\OTP\Stores\InMemoryRecoveryCodeStore;

$codes = new RecoveryCodes(new InMemoryRecoveryCodeStore());

$generated = $codes->generate(
    binding: 'user-42',
    count: 10,
    length: 10,
    groupSize: 4,
);

$generated->plainCodes;
$generated->totalGenerated;
$generated->remainingCount;

docs.title: Consuming a code
docs.text:
$result = $codes->consume('user-42', $generated->plainCodes[0]);

$result->consumed;
$result->reason;
$result->remainingCount;
$result->lastUsedAt;

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• codes are displayed in grouped, user-friendly form
  • stored values are hashed before persistence
  • generating a new set replaces the old set
  • a consumed code cannot be reused

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Replay protection is intentionally pluggable.

Available contract:

  • Infocyph\OTP\Contracts\ReplayStoreInterface

Included store:

  • Infocyph\OTP\Stores\InMemoryReplayStore

docs.title: TOTP
docs.text:
• store the last accepted timestep per user or device binding
  • reject the same timestep once accepted

docs.title: HOTP
docs.text:
• store the last accepted counter
  • reject already-used counters
  • persist any resynchronized counter once accepted

docs.title: OCRA
docs.text: • reject reused challenge and counter combinations where the flow requires single use

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
use Infocyph\OTP\Stores\InMemoryReplayStore;
use Infocyph\OTP\ValueObjects\VerificationWindow;

$result = $totp->verifyWithWindow(
    $otp,
    window: new VerificationWindow(past: 1, future: 1),
    replayStore: new InMemoryReplayStore(),
    binding: 'user-42',
);

docs.title: Secrets
docs.text: OTP secrets are reversible secrets. If your application must generate future codes, hashing alone is not enough.

docs.title: Recovery codes
docs.text: Recovery codes should usually be stored hashed.

docs.title: Replay state
docs.text: Replay state can live in cache or a database depending on your durability needs.

docs.title: Generic OTP
docs.text: Generic OTP requires a caller-provided PSR-6 cache pool implementation.

docs.title: Contracts
docs.text:
The package includes contracts you can implement in your own infrastructure:

  • Infocyph\OTP\Contracts\ReplayStoreInterface
  • Infocyph\OTP\Contracts\RecoveryCodeStoreInterface
  • Infocyph\OTP\Contracts\SecretStoreInterface

docs.title: Creating an instance
docs.text:
use Infocyph\OTP\TOTP;

$totp = new TOTP($secret, digitCount: 6, period: 30);

docs.title: Basic verification
docs.text: $totp->verify($otp, timestamp: time(), pastWindows: 1, futureWindows: 1);

docs.title: Rich verification results
docs.text:
use Infocyph\OTP\ValueObjects\VerificationWindow;

$result = $totp->verifyWithWindow(
    $otp,
    timestamp: time(),
    window: new VerificationWindow(past: 1, future: 0),
);

$result->matched;
$result->matchedTimestep;
$result->driftOffset;
$result->isExact();
$result->isDrifted();

docs.title: Time helpers
docs.text:
$totp->getCurrentTimeStep();
$totp->getRemainingSeconds();
$totp->getTimeStepFromTimestamp(1716532624);

docs.title: Replay protection
docs.text:
use Infocyph\OTP\Stores\InMemoryReplayStore;
use Infocyph\OTP\ValueObjects\VerificationWindow;

$result = $totp->verifyWithWindow(
    $otp,
    window: new VerificationWindow(past: 1, future: 1),
    replayStore: new InMemoryReplayStore(),
    binding: 'user-42',
);

docs.title: Secret rotation helper
docs.text:
$rotation = $totp->rotateSecret($newSecret, gracePeriodInSeconds: 3600);

$rotation['current'];
$rotation['next'];
$rotation['overlapUntil'];
